title: Landolphia glandulosa
intro: Landolphia glandulosa, also known as African Bloodwood or African Yellow, is a species of evergreen tree native to tropical Africa, characterized by its dark brown bark and fragrant yellow flowers. The health benefits of Landolphia glandulosa include its use as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and treating various infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenes, such as landolophine and landolophilin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Landolphia glandulosa is used to prepare herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Landolphia glandulosa belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family comprises over 5,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Landolphia glandulosa is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree, native to tropical Africa. The Apocynaceae family is known for its diverse range of species, from medicinal plants to toxic and poisonous ones. The classification of Landolphia glandulosa reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment.
uses: Landolphia glandulosa is a plant species with reported medicinal uses. Its extracts have been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Landolphia glandulosa for medicinal purposes. In some African cultures, the plant is used as a treatment for malaria and other diseases.

side_effects: Landolphia glandulosa, also known as the "African tree of death", is a highly toxic plant with severe side effects if ingested. Ingesting any part of the plant can lead to cardiac arrest, respiratory failure, and even death. The toxic compound found in the plant, called yohimbine, can also cause anxiety, tremors, and increased heart rate. If ingested in large quantities, Landolphia glandulosa can also cause seizures, hallucinations, and coma. The plant's toxic properties make it extremely hazardous to handle or consume in any form.
